zh.lifeSummary: 當John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth於1418年出生在蘇格蘭貝維其可爾波爾瓦特時，他的父親John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II21歲，母親Isobel de Home18歲。他和Katherine de Home至少有2個女兒。他去世於1481年5月8日在蘇格蘭洛包爾聖 波斯維爾斯享年63歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth于1418年出生在苏格兰貝維其可爾波爾瓦特时，他的父亲John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II21岁，母亲Isobel de Home18岁。他和Katherine de Home至少有2个女儿。他去世于1481年5月8日在苏格兰洛包爾聖 波斯維爾斯享年63岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Clair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the male personal name Clair (from Latin Clarus ‘illustrious’), which was borne by two early Christian saints, a 3rd-century bishop of Nantes and the 4th-century St. Clair of Aquitaine.
French: nickname for a cheerful individual, from the adjective clair ‘bright, light’. Compare Leclair 1.
American shortened form of French   Leclair   1, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Leclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Emile, Normand, Gaston, Adrien, Antoine, Lucien, Monique, Philias, Camille.

French: nickname for a cheerful individual, a variant of Clair 2 ‘bright, light’, with fused masculine definite article le. Derivation from the male personal name Clair 1 is also possible. Compare Laclair , Laclaire , and Leclear .
French: altered form of   Leclerc   1.

variants.name: Klar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ezriel, Tzvi, Baruch, Devorah, Isadore, Nachman, Yitzchok.  German Gerhard, Johann, Erhard, Kurt, Willibald.
German: nickname from Middle High German and Middle Low German klār ‘pure, beautiful, clear’.
German: from the female personal name Klara, an equivalent of Clara (see Clair and Sinclair ).

variants.name: Cler
meanings: French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Clarin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luz, Alejandro, Armida, Epifano, Florencio, Jose, Libia, Librada, Maria Luisa, Raul, Santos, Sergio.

Swedish: variant of Klarin, ornamental or perhaps habitational name formed with klar ‘clear’ (possibly representing an element of a placename) + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Clair  .

variants.name: Auclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Raoul, Andre, Fernand, Ovila, Alphonse, Cecile, Gaston, Germaine, Jacques, Jean-Paul.
French: patronymic from Clair , from (fils) au Clair ‘(son) of Clair’. Compare Leclerc 2 and Oclair .

History: Pierre and André Auclair, two brothers from Saint-Vivien near La Rochelle in France, are recorded in Charlesbourg, QC, in 1679 and 1681, respectively, the years of their marriage. The descendants of one or another brother also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Leclerc, Oclair, and O'Clair.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth was born in 1418, in Polwarth, Berwickshire, Scotland, his father, John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II, was 21 and his mother, Isobel de Home, was 18. He had at least 2 daughters with Katherine de Home. He died on 8 May 1481, in Saint Boswells, Roxburghshire, Scotland, at the age of 63, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth est né en 1418, à Polwarth, Berwickshire, Écosse, son père, John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Isobel de Home, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Katherine de Home. Il est décédé le 8 mai 1481, à Saint Boswells, Roxburghshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth 1418 in Polwarth, Berwickshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II 21 und seine Mutter Isobel de Home 18. Er hatte mit Katherine de Home mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 8. Mai 1481 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Saint Boswells, Roxburghshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth è nato il nel 1418 nel Polwarth, Berwickshire, Scotland, il suo padre, John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Isobel de Home, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Katherine de Home. Morì il 8 maggio 1481, nel Saint Boswells, Roxburghshire, Scotland, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarthが1418年にスコットランドバーウィクシュアイアポルワースで生まれた時，父John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth IIは21歳，母Isobel de Homeは18歳であった。彼はKatherine de Homeとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1481年5月8日にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアサイント ボスウエルズで63歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth(이)가 1418년 스컷랜드 버윅실르 포워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Isobel de Home(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Katherine de Home(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1481년 5월 8일 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 세인트 보스웰스에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth nasceu em 1418, em Polwarth, Berwickshire, Scotland, seu pai, John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Isobel de Home, tinha 18 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Katherine de Home. Ele faleceu em 8 de maio de 1481, em Saint Boswells, Roxburghshire, Scotland, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth родился в 1418 г. в местности полварт, бирвиккшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Isobel de Home, 18. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Katherine de Home. Он умер 8 мая 1481 г. в местности сеинт босвеллс, роксбургшир, Шотландия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John St Clair of Herdmanston and Polwarth nació en 1418, en Polwarth, Berwickshire, Scotland, su papá, John de St. Clair - Laird of Polwerth II, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Isobel de Home, tenía 18 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Katherine de Home. Murió el 8 de mayo de 1481, en Saint Boswells, Roxburghshire, Scotland, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
